(born 1979) was adviser to the [Prime Minister of Turkey](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Turkey "Prime Minister of Turkey"). Born in [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul"), his origins are from [Denizli Province](/wiki/Denizli_Province "Denizli Province") in south-western Turkey.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

["بالصور: قصة "طه".. من أزقة المدينة المنورة إلى مجلس الوزراء التركي"](http://www.mbc.net/ar/programs/mbc-news/ksa/articles/%D8%A8%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%B5%D9%88%D8%B1--%D9%82%D8%B5%D8%A9--%D8%B7%D9%87----%D9%85%D9%86-%D8%A3%D8%B2%D9%82%D8%A9-%D8%A7%D9%84%D9%85%D8%AF%D9%8A%D9%86%D8%A9-%D8%A7%D9%84%D9%85%D9%86%D9%88%D8%B1%D8%A9-%D8%A5%D9%84%D9%89-%D9%85%D8%AC%D9%84%D8%B3-%D8%A7%D9%84%D9%88%D8%B2%D8%B1%D8%A7%D8%A1-%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%AA%D8%B1%D9%83%D9%8A/photos/206649.html). *www.mbc.net* (in Arabic). Retrieved 2018-01-16.

**Taha Genç** (born 1979) was adviser to the [Prime Minister of Turkey](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Turkey "Prime Minister of Turkey"). Born in [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul"), his origins are from [Denizli Province](/wiki/Denizli_Province "Denizli Province") in south-western Turkey.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

He moved with his family to [Medina](/wiki/Medina "Medina"), [Saudi Arabia](/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia"). He continued his primary education at school of Sakkaf. Also, he completed his middle school at Abdullah Ibn Abbas School. He is fluent in Arabic language, in addition to the local dialects.

After returning to Turkey, he completed his university career in [İzmir](/wiki/%C4%B0zmir "İzmir") at [Dokuz Eylül University](/wiki/Dokuz_Eyl%C3%BCl_University "Dokuz Eylül University"), then returned to Istanbul and received a master's degree in International Relations from [Istanbul University](/wiki/Istanbul_University "Istanbul University"). Genc started the field of consulting since his youth; he began his career as an adviser to the prime minister since 10 years ago.[*[when?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*]

Genc is an activist on [social media](/wiki/Social_media "Social media") platforms and is known as "Taha El-Turki" in the Arab countries. He is one of the few political figures who communicate directly with his followers from different countries in the world and from the Arab world in particular.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Genc defines Turkish culture and teaches some Turkish words and phrases to his followers. He is also an amateur photographer, and all photographs and videos which he published on the social media are taken by himself.
